tower.title: 412 metres of architectural ambition
tower.desc: Kuwait's tallest building — designed by SOM, clad in Jura limestone, engineered as a single sculptural gesture.

architecture.title: Glass toward the Gulf. Stone toward the desert.
architecture.desc: Two hyperbolic paraboloid walls, a 24-metre column-free lamella, and 258,000 m² of Jura limestone cladding.

business.title: An address for the region's principals
business.desc: Ministries, sovereign funds, legal & financial leaders, global luxury brands — tenants by invitation.

leasing.title: Configurations tailored to your mandate
leasing.desc: 450 m² half-floors to 2,300 m² full floors. Executive suites on 74 & 75. Highest address in Kuwait.

gulf.caption: Visible from every shore of the city. The landmark that orients you.
gulf.alt: Al Hamra Tower above Kuwait City at dusk, Arabian Gulf on the horizon

architecture.headlinePrimary: A form born from sun, wind, and the Gulf.
architecture.bodyPrimaryPara1: The Al Hamra Tower's geometry was generated by a single decisive act: subtracting a spiraling quadrant from a simple prismatic volume, then rotating it incrementally at each higher level. The result is two hyperbolic paraboloid walls — the iconic flared ribbons — that extend the full 412 metres of the building's height.
architecture.bodyPrimaryPara2Html: The transparent glass envelope faces north, west, and east — toward the Arabian Gulf. The monolithic stone wall faces south — shielding every office from Kuwait's desert sun. Architecture and engineering evolved together. The structure is the form.
architecture.pullquote: Like an enshrouded figure with its delicate glass veil, the Tower provides spectacular views over much of Kuwait and across the Arabian Gulf.
architecture.headlineSecondary: Glass toward the Gulf. Stone toward the desert.

invitation.title: An address you're invited to inhabit.
invitation.body: Al Hamra Business Tower does not advertise availability. Tenancy is by invitation — a quiet conversation between the building and the institution worthy of it.

globalRecognition.title: Engineered to be seen — engineered to be remembered.
globalRecognition.body: Cityscape Award 2010 · Chicago Athenaeum · MIPIM Architectural Review · Miami Architectural Biennial 2007. The world's largest stone-clad skyscraper — 258,000m² of Jura limestone — engineered by SOM San Francisco.

unmatchedAddress.title: The Capital's Hub
unmatchedAddress.body: Sharq District — Kuwait's financial and government quarter. Direct access to the National Bank of Kuwait, major ministries, and the waterfront. 2,000 parking spaces across 11 levels. Fibre optic smart building. 100% power redundancy.

floorConfigs.title: Space that adapts to the institution inside.

c1.label: Configuration 01
c1.title: Ministerial Suite
c1.body: Corner office configuration with panoramic city views and private meeting room. Ideal for regional offices and professional firms.

c2.label: Configuration 02
c2.title: Sovereign Floor
c2.body: Entire floor exclusivity with private elevator access and dedicated reception. 3.2m ceiling height with 360° views.

c3.label: Configuration 03
c3.title: Flagship Headquarters
c3.body: Multiple floors with building signage rights and dedicated parking levels. Kuwait's premier headquarters address.
